items.situation: two students discussing a new restaurant downtown and which dish to try

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that new place, Maple Spoon, downtown yet?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not yet. I heard the noodle bowls are amazing but the line at lunch is brutal.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Skip the lunch rush. Go around three in the afternoon. I had the spicy peanut noodles and they were unreal — best meal I've had all month.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sold. Was anything overpriced or just kind of average?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, the desserts. They look pretty but taste flat. Get the noodles, then grab coffee somewhere else.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good tip. I'll head over this Sunday.

questions.stem: Which dish does the woman recommend?

options.A: The mushroom dumplings on the appetizer menu
options.B: The spicy peanut noodles she ordered there
options.C: The seasonal dessert with a maple sauce
options.D: The grilled fish bowl with brown rice

questions.explanation: 女生明确说她点的“spicy peanut noodles”是这个月最好的一餐，所以推荐这道菜。A、D 完全没提及；C 与对话相反，她说甜点中看不中吃。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's overall opinion of Maple Spoon?

options.A: She thinks the restaurant is overpriced and rarely worth visiting
options.B: She believes the lunch hour is the best time to dine there
options.C: She regrets recommending the place to her classmate
options.D: She considers the main dishes better than the desserts

questions.explanation: 她大力推荐面条但批评甜点“taste flat”，因此她认为主菜比甜点好。A 她只批评甜点，不是整家店；B 她建议避开午餐高峰；C 她主动推荐并未表现后悔。

items.situation: two students at the rec center talking about a free intro yoga class

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hey, did you sign up for that free intro yoga class at the rec center yet?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I tried this morning, but the website said the session was full.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Really? They added a second slot on Thursday at 7:00 PM. Check again.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, nice. Do I need to bring my own mat?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, no rentals this time. There's a sign-up form at the front desk too if the website acts up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll swing by after my chem lab.

questions.stem: What does the man tell the woman about Thursday?

options.A: A new yoga instructor will lead the class
options.B: A registration fee was added that morning
options.C: A second class session was opened that day
options.D: A mat rental program was launched recently

questions.explanation: 男生说“他们周四晚上 7:00 加了第二场”，所以那天新开了一节课。A、B、D 在对话中均未出现。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Visit the rec center after her chemistry lab
options.B: Buy a yoga mat at the campus bookstore
options.C: Email the front desk to request a refund
options.D: Wait until the morning session reopens

questions.explanation: 女生最后说“化学实验课后我顺路过去”，且男生提到前台也能报名，所以她最可能化学课后去 rec center 报名。B 她从未说要新买垫子；C 没有退款话题；D 上午的课她说不上。

items.situation: two friends figuring out a bus or train schedule for a day trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, did you figure out how we're getting to Riverton on Saturday? I checked the bus schedule and the express only runs at seven in the morning.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Seven? Yikes, that's brutal.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I know. But if we take the regular bus, it stops everywhere and gets there around noon. We'd barely have any time at the festival.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, what about the train? My roommate said there's a new weekend train that leaves at nine.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? That sounds way better. Is it expensive?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's twelve dollars one way with a student ID. I'll book the tickets tonight through the rail app.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect, I'll pay you back tomorrow.

questions.stem: What is the problem with the express bus?

options.A: It departs very early in the morning
options.B: It costs more than the train ticket
options.C: It does not run on Saturdays
options.D: It is fully booked for the weekend

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生明确说快车只在早上七点发车，男生反应说"太残酷了"，说明问题在于出发时间太早。B错误：对话从未比较快车和火车的价格。C错误：女生说的是发车时间，没说不在周六运营。D错误：对话中没有提到座位售罄的问题。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do tonight?

options.A: Take the regular bus to Riverton
options.B: Ask his roommate for a ride to the station
options.C: Reserve train tickets through a mobile app
options.D: Cancel the trip to the festival

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生明确说"今晚我会通过铁路应用订票"，因此他最可能做的就是用手机应用订火车票。A错误：男生和女生已决定不坐公交，普通公交是被否决的选项。B错误：对话中虽然提到室友，但只是室友告诉他有这趟火车，不涉及让室友送他去车站。D错误：他们正在积极规划去节日，没有任何取消行程的迹象。

items.situation: two students talking about a new restaurant downtown and which dish to try

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So you finally tried Beacon Street? I've been hearing nonstop hype about it. Was it actually worth the wait?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, the atmosphere's gorgeous, but the menu is hit or miss. The pasta everyone raves about online? Pretty average.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. So what should I order if I go?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Skip the pasta and go straight for the duck confit. It's the only dish the chef actually trained on, and you can tell.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Duck? I usually wouldn't pick that, but I trust you.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Trust me, it's the move. Oh, and the desserts come from a bakery next door, so don't waste room on those.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good to know. I'll grab dessert somewhere else after.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Smart. There's a little gelato shop two blocks over that's incredible.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the conversation?

options.A: To complain about the high prices at a new restaurant
options.B: To share advice about what to order at a restaurant
options.C: To decide whether to eat downtown this weekend
options.D: To recommend a bakery and gelato shop nearby

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：整段对话围绕男生告诉女生在这家新餐厅应该点什么菜（鸭肉而不是意面）以及避开什么（甜点）。A错误：对话从未提到价格高的问题。C错误：女生已经决定要去，不是在犹豫是否去市中心吃饭。D错误：甜点店和冰淇淋店只是次要细节，不是对话主旨。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's plan after dinner?

options.A: She will return to Beacon Street another night
options.B: She will order the pasta despite the man's warning
options.C: She will write an online review of the restaurant
options.D: She will buy dessert at a different location

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：女生明确说"我会在别的地方买甜点"，男生还推荐了两个街区外的冰淇淋店，所以她饭后会去别的地方买甜点。A错误：对话中没有任何关于她再次光顾Beacon Street的暗示，是过度推理。B错误：她说"我信你"，表示会听男生的建议，意面是被否决的选项。C错误：对话中没有提到写评论，与对话无关。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally saw that thriller you've been talking about. The Quiet Hour.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh nice! What did you think?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, the twists were brilliant. I didn't see the ending coming at all, and the second one in the middle? Total shock.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right? That's why I keep recommending it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: But, wow, it's long. Almost three hours. There's a whole subplot with the sister that they could've cut entirely.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I'll admit that part dragged. I actually checked my watch twice.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Same. I might rewatch it sometime, but I'd skip ahead through the sister scenes.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Smart. There's actually a fan edit online that trims about forty minutes. I'll send you the link.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes, please do!

questions.stem: What specific part of the movie did the woman think should have been cut?

options.A: The opening sequence of the film
options.B: The final twist near the end
options.C: A subplot involving a sister character
options.D: A long flashback in the middle

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生明确说"姐姐那条副线他们完全可以剪掉"，所以她认为应该删除涉及姐姐这个角色的副线。A错误：对话从未提到开场镜头有问题。B错误：女生其实非常赞美结尾的反转，认为它出人意料。D错误：对话中没有提到中段的闪回情节，这是听起来合理但未提及的细节。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do after the conversation?

options.A: Send the woman a link to a shorter fan edit
options.B: Rewatch the movie with the woman this weekend
options.C: Write a negative review about the movie's length
options.D: Stop recommending the movie to other friends

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生明确说"我会把链接发给你"，指的是网上一个删减了约四十分钟的影迷版本。B错误：对话中没有提到他们要一起重看这部电影，是过度推理。C错误：男生承认有部分拖沓，但他仍然在推荐这部电影，写差评违背他的态度。D错误：他明显仍然欣赏这部电影并继续向他人推荐，与对话信息相反。

items.situation: two friends discussing whether to join a campus club or intramural team

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, the activities fair ended yesterday and I still can't decide. Should I sign up for the photography club or the intramural soccer team?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, both meet on Tuesdays, right? You can't really do both.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. And I already have my chemistry lab on Thursday nights, so I need to be careful.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, what are you actually hoping to get out of it? Like, friends? Exercise? A new skill?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, I want to meet people outside my major. The chem crowd's nice but kind of intense.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then soccer, hands down. The intramural league is super social, and they go out together every weekend.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Even if I'm not great at soccer?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Totally. Half the team had never played before this year. It's all about showing up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, I'm convinced. I'll register tonight.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to balance lab work with social activities
options.B: Whether photography club fees are worth paying
options.C: The best way to make friends outside one's major
options.D: Which extracurricular activity the woman should join

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：整段对话围绕女生在摄影社和校内足球队之间犹豫不决，男生帮她做选择。A错误：化学实验只是被简单提及作为时间冲突，不是讨论的主题。B错误：对话中完全没有提到会费问题。C错误：交友是她选择活动的动机之一，但这是太狭窄的视角，主题是选哪个活动。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's soccer ability?

options.A: She played competitively in high school
options.B: She has limited experience with the sport
options.C: She refuses to join because of her skill level
options.D: She is the team's most talented new member

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生问"即使我足球踢得不好也可以吗？"暗示她经验有限，所以可以推断她对这项运动经验不多。A错误：她担心自己技术不好，与高中竞技踢球矛盾，与对话信息相反。C错误：男生说不用紧张后她答应报名，她并没有拒绝加入，被否决的选项。D错误：她担心自己不擅长，称她是队里最有天赋的新成员是过度推理。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening or art class they took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, didn't you take that weekend pottery class at the community center last month? How was it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, I loved it. Way more relaxing than I expected. The instructor was really patient.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I was thinking about signing up. What did you actually make?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We started with simple bowls the first weekend, then moved on to mugs. Mine came out a little crooked, but it works.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Aww, that's so cute. Was it expensive?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sixty bucks for four sessions, and that includes all the clay and glazes. Pretty good deal.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's reasonable. Do I need to bring anything?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just wear old clothes. Trust me, you'll get clay everywhere.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. I'll register this afternoon.

questions.stem: What was the first item the man made in the pottery class?

options.A: A simple bowl
options.B: A crooked coffee mug
options.C: A glazed dinner plate
options.D: A small clay vase

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生明确说"我们第一个周末从简单的碗开始"，所以他做的第一件东西是简单的碗。B错误：杯子是第二个周末做的，不是第一件，是混淆细节。C错误：对话中没有提到盘子，听起来合理但未提及。D错误：对话中没有提到花瓶，听起来合理但未提及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do this afternoon?

options.A: Go shopping for new clothes to wear
options.B: Ask the man to attend the class with her
options.C: Sign up for the pottery class herself
options.D: Visit the community center to see the studio

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生在对话最后明确说"我下午就去报名"，因此她下午最可能做的事是给陶艺课报名。A错误：男生让她穿旧衣服，而不是去买新衣服，与男生的建议相反。B错误：对话中没有提到邀请男生一起上课，与对话无关。D错误：她说要去报名，没说要先去社区中心实地参观，是过度推理。

items.situation: two students debating which library floor is best for studying

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, where do you usually study now? Third floor or basement?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, the third floor's gotten too noisy. They added group tables.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, really? I haven't been since last spring.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Try the basement. It's a silent zone, and there are private carrels by the windows.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Carrels by windows? That sounds perfect for my thesis work.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just grab one before two. They fill up fast after that.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. I'll head there tomorrow morning.

questions.stem: What does the man say has changed on the third floor?

options.A: It has become noisier than before
options.B: It has added new vending machines
options.C: It has limited hours for finals
options.D: It has lost its window seating area

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生明确说三楼变得太吵，因为加了小组讨论桌。B错误：对话从未提到自动售货机。C错误：对话没提期末考试期间时间受限。D错误：对话只提到地下室有窗户旁的隔间，没说三楼失去窗座。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tomorrow morning?

options.A: Move her thesis work back upstairs as before
options.B: Reserve a group table for her thesis discussion
options.C: Head to the basement to find a private carrel
options.D: Continue studying in her dorm room as usual

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生最后说"明天早上我会去那里"，结合男生让她在两点前到地下室抢隔间，可推断她会去地下室找隔间。A错误：三楼正是被否决的选项，她不会回去。B错误：小组桌正是导致三楼吵闹的原因，她不会去预订。D错误：对话中没有讨论待在宿舍的方案。

items.situation: two friends planning to split a textbook cost for a chemistry course

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Did you see how much the chemistry textbook costs this semester? Almost two hundred dollars.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yikes. I was thinking we could split a used copy and share notes through the term.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Actually, that could work. We have the same lab section anyway, so we'd be on the same pace.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right, and the library has one on reserve too, in case we both need it the same night.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good backup plan. Let's check the bookstore's used section after lunch today.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sounds great. I'll bring my student ID for the discount.

questions.stem: What is the woman's main suggestion to the man?

options.A: Buy a brand-new copy from the campus bookstore directly
options.B: Share a used textbook and split the cost
options.C: Borrow detailed notes from a previous classmate
options.D: Sign up for a totally different lab section

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生明确提出"我们可以合买一本二手书然后共享笔记"。A错误：对话中没有提到全新书，反而是买二手。C错误：对话从未提及向以前的同学借笔记。D错误：他们已经在同一个实验班，不需要换班。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the library copy of the textbook?

options.A: It is currently checked out by another student all term
options.B: It can be borrowed for the whole semester at once
options.C: It is missing several important chapters in the book
options.D: It serves as a backup when their copy is taken

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：女生提到图书馆有一本作为备用，"以防我们两个同一晚都需要它"，所以图书馆的书是他们共享本不可用时的备用方案。A错误：对话没说图书馆的书被借走。B错误：对话没提整学期可借。C错误：对话从未提到缺章。

items.situation: two roommates deciding how to decorate their dorm for the holidays

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So, are we doing anything for the dorm before break? Last year was so plain.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I was thinking some string lights and a small fake tree from the dollar store.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's cute. But weren't string lights banned in dorms because of the wiring issue?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, you're right. The RA said battery-operated ones are okay though.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll grab a set at the campus store tomorrow.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: And I'll figure out the tree situation this weekend.

questions.stem: What does the man originally suggest buying?

options.A: A large fabric wreath for the dorm door
options.B: A pair of decorative pillows from the bookstore
options.C: A string of lights and a small tree
options.D: A scented candle set for the study desks

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：男生说"我在想灯串和一棵小假树"，所以最初的建议就是灯串和小树。A错误：对话没提花环。B错误：男生提的是dollar store，不是bookstore，且没提枕头。D错误：对话从未提到香薰蜡烛。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about plug-in string lights?

options.A: They are not permitted in the dorm rooms
options.B: They are sold only at off-campus stores
options.C: They are too expensive for most students
options.D: They are difficult to install on dorm walls

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：女生提到灯串因为线路问题在宿舍被禁，男生确认"你说得对，电池式的才被允许"，可推断插电式灯串不被允许。B错误：对话没提销售地点。C错误：价格从未讨论。D错误：安装难度未涉及。
